subcommands.name: icat
subcommands.description: A cat like utility to display images in the terminal

name: --align
options.description: Horizontal alignment for the displayed image

name: --place
options.description: Choose where on the screen to display the image

name: --scale-up
options.description: Images that are smaller than the specified area to be scaled up to use as much of the specified area as possible

name: --background
options.description: Specify a background color, this will cause transparent images to be composited on top of the specified color

name: --mirror
options.description: Mirror the image about a horizontal or vertical axis or both

name: --clear
options.description: Remove all images currently displayed on the screen

name: --transfer-mode
options.description: Which mechanism to use to transfer images to the terminal

name: --detect-support
options.description: Detect support for image display in the terminal

name: --detection-timeout
options.description: How long to wait for detection to complete before aborting

name: --print-window-size
options.description: Print the current terminal window size in pixels

name: --stdin
options.description: Read an image from stdin

name: -A
--after-context
options.description: Shows NUM lines after each match

name: --auto-hybrid-regex
options.description: When this flag is used, ripgrep will dynamically choose between supported regex engines depending on the features used in a pattern

name: -B
--before-context
options.description: Shows NUM lines before each match

name: --binary
options.description: Enabling this flag will cause ripgrep to search binary files

name: --block-buffered
options.description: When enabled, ripgrep will use block buffering

name: -b
--byte-offset
options.description: Prints the 0-based byte offset within the input file before each line of output

name: -s
--case-sensitive
options.description: Search case sensitively

name: --color
options.description: Controls when to use colors

suggestions.name: always
suggestions.description: Colors will always be used regardless of where output is sent

suggestions.name: ansi
suggestions.description: Colors will always be used, but emits ANSI escapes

name: --colors
options.description: Specifies color settings for use in the output

name: --column
options.description: Shows column numbers for the first match on each line

name: -C
--context
options.description: Shows NUM lines before and after each match

name: --context-separator
options.description: The string used to separate non-contiguous context lines in the output

name: -c
--count
options.description: Suppresses normal output and shows the number of lines that match the given pattersns for each file searched

name: --count-matches
options.description: Suppresses normal output and shows the number of individual matches of the given patterns for each file searched

name: --crlf
options.description: Treats CRLF as a line terminator instead of just a line break

name: --debug
options.description: Shows debug messages

name: --dfa-size-limit
options.description: The upper size limit of the regex DFA

name: -E
--encoding
options.description: Specifies the text encoding that ripgrep will use on all files searched

name: --engine
options.description: Specifies which regular expression engine to use

name: -f
--file
options.description: Searches for patterns from the given file, with one pattern per line

name: --files
options.description: Prints each file that would be searched without actually performing the search

name: -l
--files-with-matches
options.description: Only prints the paths with at least one match

name: --files-without-match
options.description: Only prints the paths that contains zero matches

name: -F
--fixed-strings
options.description: Treats the pattern as a literal string instead of a regular expression

name: -L
--follow
options.description: Follows symbolic links while traversing directories

name: -g
--glob
options.description: Includes or excludes files and directories for searching that match the given glob

name: --glob-case-insensitive
options.description: Processes glob patterns given with the -g/--glob flag

name: --heading
options.description: Prints the file path above clusters of matches from each file instead of printing the file path as a prefix for reach matched line

name: --hidden
options.description: Searches hidden files and directories

name: --iglob
options.description: Includes or excludes files and directories for searching that match given the glob

name: --ignore-file
options.description: Specifies a path to one or more .gitignore format rules files

name: --ignore-file-case-insensitive
options.description: Process ignores files case insensitively

name: --include-zero
options.description: Prints the number of matchesfor each file when used with --count or --count matches

name: -v
--invert-match
options.description: Inverts matching

name: --json
options.description: Enables printing results in a JSON Lines format

name: --no-json
options.description: Disables the JSON Lines format

name: --line-buffered
options.description: Enables line buffering

name: -n
--line-number
options.description: Shows the line numbers (1-based)

name: -x
--line-regexp
options.description: Only shows matches surrounded by line boundaries

name: -M
--max-columns
options.description: Don't print lines longer than this limit in bytes

name: --max-columns-preview
options.description: Completely replaces any line that is too long with a message indicating that a matching line was removed

name: -m
--max-count
options.description: Limits the number of matching lines per file searched to NUM

name: --max-depth
options.description: Limits the depth of directory traversal to NUM levels beyond the paths given

name: --max-filesize
options.description: Ignores files larger than NUM in size

name: -mmap
options.description: Searches using memory maps when possible

name: -U
--multiline
options.description: Enables matching across multiple lines

name: --multiline-dotall
options.description: Enables dot all in your regex pattern

name: --no-config
options.description: Never read configuration files

name: -I
--no-filename
options.description: Never prints the file path with the matched lines

name: --no-heading
options.description: Don't group matches by each file

name: --no-ignore
options.description: Don't respect ignore files (.gitignore, .ignore, etc.)

name: --no-ignore-dot
options.description: Don't respect .ignore files

name: --no-ignore-excluded
options.description: Don't respect ignore files that are manually configured for the repository

name: --no-ignore-files
options.description: Ignores any --ignore-file flags

name: --no-ignore-global
options.description: Don't respect ignore files that come from `global` sources

name: --no-ignore-messages
options.description: Suppresses all error messages related to parsing ignore files

name: --no-ignore-parent
options.description: Don't repsect ignore files in parent directories

name: --no-ignore-vcs
options.description: Don't respect version control ignore files

name: -N
--no-line-number
options.description: Suppress line numbers

name: --no-messages
options.description: Suppresses all error messages related to opening and reading files

name: --no-map
options.description: Never use memory maps, even when they might be faster

name: --no-require-git
options.description: Will only respect global gitignore rules

name: --no-unicode
options.description: Turn off unicode

name: --unicode
options.description: Disables --no-unicode flag

name: --null
options.description: Whenever a file path is printed, follow it with a NUL byte

name: --null-data
options.description: Uses NUL as a line terminator instead of the default line break

name: --one-file-system
options.description: Will not cross file system boundaries relative to where the search started from

name: --no-one-file-system
options.description: Disables `--one-file-system` flag

name: -o
--only-matching
options.description: Prints only the matched (non-empty) parts of a matching line, with each such part on a separate output line

name: --passthru
options.description: Prints both matching and non-matching lines

name: --path-separator
options.description: Sets the path separator to use when printing file paths

name: -P
--pcre2
options.description: Usess the PCRE2 regex engine instead of its default regex engine

name: --pcre2-version
options.description: Prints the version of PCRE2 in use

name: --pre
options.description: For each input File, search the standard output of the command

name: --pre-glob
options.description: Only files that match the given set of globs will be handed to the command specified by the `--pre flag`

name: -p
--pretty
options.description: Convenience alias for `--color always --heading --line-number`

name: -q
--quiet
options.description: Do not print anything to stdout

name: --regex-size-limit
options.description: Sets the upper size limit of the compiled regex

name: -e
--regexp
options.description: A pattern to search for

name: -r
--replace
options.description: Replace every match with the text given when printing results

name: -z
--search-zip
options.description: Search in compressed files

name: -S
--smart-case
options.description: Searches case insensitively if the pattern is all lowercase

suggestions.name: none
suggestions.description: Do not sort results. Fastest. Can be multi-threaded

suggestions.name: path
suggestions.description: Sort by file path, Always single-threaded

suggestions.name: modified
suggestions.description: Sort by the last modified time on a file. Always single-threaded

suggestions.name: accessed
suggestions.description: Sort by the last accessed time on a file. Always single-threaded

suggestions.name: created
suggestions.description: Sort by the creation time on a file. Always single-threaded

name: --sortr
options.description: Enables sorting results in descending order

suggestions.name: none
suggestions.description: Do not sort results. Fastest. Can be multi-threaded

suggestions.name: path
suggestions.description: Sort by file path, Always single-threaded

suggestions.name: modified
suggestions.description: Sort by the last modified time on a file. Always single-threaded

suggestions.name: accessed
suggestions.description: Sort by the last accessed time on a file. Always single-threaded

suggestions.name: created
suggestions.description: Sort by the creation time on a file. Always single-threaded

name: --stats
options.description: Prints aggregate statistics about this ripgrep search

name: -a
--text
options.description: Searches binary files as if they were text

name: -j
--threads
options.description: The approximate number of threads to use

name: --trim
options.description: All ASCII whitespace at the beginning of each line printed will be trimmed

name: -t
--type
options.description: Only searches files matching TYPE

name: --type-add
options.description: Add a new glob for a particular file type

name: --type-clear
options.description: Clear the file type globs previously defined for type

name: --type-list
options.description: Shows all supported file types and their corresponding globs

name: -T
--type-not
options.description: Do not search files matching type

name: -u
--unrestricted
options.description: Reduce the level of smart searching, increase by adding `u`s, ex: -uuu

name: --vimgrep
options.description: Shows results with every match on its own line, including line numbers and column nnumbers

name: -H
--with-filename
options.description: Displays the file path for matches

name: -w
--word-regexp
options.description: Only shows the matches surrounded by word boundaries

args.name: Pattern
args.description: A regular expression used for searching

args.name: Path
args.description: A file or directory to search

options.description: Forces ssh to try protocol version 1 only
Forces ssh to try protocol version 2 only
Forces ssh to use IPv4 addresses only
Forces ssh to use IPv6 addresses only

name: -A
options.description: Enables forwarding of the authentication agent connection

name: -a
options.description: Disables forwarding of the authentication agent connection

name: -b
options.description: Use bind_address on the local machine as the source address of the connection

name: -C
options.description: Requests compression of all data (including stdin, stdout, stderr, and data for forwarded X11 and TCP connections)

name: -c
options.description: Selects the cipher specification for encrypting the session

name: -D
options.description: Specifies a local 'dynamic' application-level port forwarding

name: -e
options.description: Sets the escape character for sessions with a pty (default: '~')

name: -F
options.description: Specifies an alternative per-user configuration file

name: -f
options.description: Requests ssh to go to background just before command execution

name: -g
options.description: Allows remote hosts to connect to local forwarded ports

name: -I
options.description: Specify the PKCS#11 shared library ssh should use to communicate with a PKCS#11 token providing the user's private RSA key

name: -i
options.description: Selects a file from which the identity (private key) for public key authentication is read

name: -K
options.description: Enables GSSAPI-based authentication and forwarding (delegation) of GSSAPI credentials to the server

name: -k
options.description: Disables forwarding (delegation) of GSSAPI credentials to the server

name: -L
options.description: Specifies that the given port on the local (client) host is to be forwarded to the given host and port on the remote side

args.name: port:host:hostport
args.description: Local port, followed by host and host port to forward to

name: -l
options.description: Specifies the user to log in as on the remote machine

args.name: login name
args.description: Name of user logging into remote machine

name: -M
options.description: Places the ssh client into ``master'' mode for connection sharing

name: -m
options.description: Additionally, for protocol version 2 a comma-separated list of MAC (message authentication code) algorithms can be specified in order of preference

name: -N
options.description: Do not execute a remote command

name: -n
options.description: Redirects stdin from /dev/null (actually, prevents reading from stdin)

name: -O
options.description: Control an active connection multiplexing master process

args.name: ctl cmd
args.description: Command that's passed to the master process

name: -o
options.description: Can be used to give options in the format used in the configuration file

args.name: option
args.description: Options that can be specified in the format of the config file

name: -p
options.description: Port to connect to on the remote host

name: -q
options.description: Quiet mode.  Causes most warning and diagnostic messages to be suppressed

name: -R
options.description: Specifies that the given port on the remote (server) host is to be forwarded to the given host and port on the local side

args.name: port:host:hostport
args.description: Local port, followed by host and host port to forward to

name: -S
options.description: Specifies the location of a control socket for connection sharing, or the string 'none' to disable connection sharing

name: -s
options.description: May be used to request invocation of a subsystem on the remote system

name: -T
options.description: Disable pseudo-tty allocation

name: -t
options.description: Force pseudo-tty allocation

name: -V
options.description: Display the version number and exit

name: -v
options.description: Verbose mode.  Causes ssh to print debugging messages about its progress

name: -W
options.description: Requests that standard input and output on the client be forwarded to host on port over the secure channel

args.name: host:port
args.description: Host and port to forward to

name: -w
options.description: Requests tunnel device forwarding with the specified tun(4) devices between the client (local_tun) and the server (remote_tun)

name: -X
options.description: Enables X11 forwarding

name: -x
options.description: Disables X11 forwarding

name: -Y
options.description: Enables trusted X11 forwarding

name: -y
options.description: Send log information using the syslog(3) system module

args.name: user@hostname
args.description: Address of remote machine to log into

name: -T
--title
options.description: Set the OS window title

name: -C
--config
options.description: Specify a path to the configuration file(s) to use

name: -o
--override
options.description: Override individual configuration options

name: -d
--directory
--working-directory
options.description: Change to the specified directory when launching

name: --session
options.description: Path to a file containing the startup session

name: --hold
options.description: Remain open after child process exits

name: --single-instance
options.description: Only a single instance of kitty will run

name: --instance-group
options.description: Kitty will open a new window in an existing instance and quit immediately

name: --wait-for-single-instance-window-close
options.description: The new window will not quit till the newly opened window is closed

name: --listen-on
options.description: Tell kitty to listen on the specified address for control messages

name: --start-as
options.description: Control how the initial kitty window is created

name: -v
--version
options.description: The current kitty version

name: -h
--help
options.description: Display this help message
